data.title: US Steel gains on report President Trump will meet with steel firm
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) hit session highs, gaining 4.3%, after a report that President Donald Trump is scheduled to meet with the steel producer today.

Trump is meeting with the leaders of US Steel (NYSE:X) and FedEx (FDX) on Thursday, according a posting on X from an ABC reporter.

The stock gained as investors are likely hoping that US Steel (X) can convince Trump to support the company's planned $14 billion sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY), which was blocked by Former President Joe Biden last month.

The meeting comes as Trump is scheduled to meet with Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba on Friday. Ishiba is expected to highlight that Japan is the biggest investor in the U.S., leading foreign direct investment for the past five years, and discuss how Japanese companies are creating American jobs in the meeting on Friday, according to a Nikkei report on Tuesday.

Earlier Thursday, it was reported that Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) said its proposed takeover of US Steel (X) matches President Trump's goal of a stronger United States.

data.title: Lyft teams up with Anthropic to improve customer service features
data.description:
Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) and AI startup Anthropic unveiled a groundbreaking partnership aimed at transforming the rideshare experience for Lyft's (NASDAQ:LYFT) network of 40 million annual riders and over 1 million drivers.

The collaboration centers on developing innovative AI-powered products that prioritize customer needs while maintaining a human-centric approach, aligning with Anthropic's reputation for creating empathetic AI solutions.

The partnership encompasses three key areas of cooperation. First, the companies will work together to develop and deploy Anthropic-powered solutions across Lyft's business operations. Second, Lyft will gain early access to research testing of new Anthropic offerings, ensuring that future capabilities align with the needs of riders and drivers. Lastly, Anthropic will provide training and education to Lyft's engineering team, preparing them to lead the next wave of innovation in the rideshare industry.

Lyft (LYFT) has already incorporated Anthropic's Claude AI model into its customer care assistant via Amazon Bedrock. The integration was noted to have yielded impressive results, reducing the average customer service resolution time by 87% and handling thousands of requests daily.

Looking down the road, the long-term collaboration aims to enhance the Lyft (LYFT) rideshare experience by leveraging AI to create innovative products that foster human connection and improve efficiency for both riders and drivers.

data.title: Molina Healthcare projects 9% revenue growth to $42B in 2025, driven by contract wins
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Molina Healthcare (NYSE:MOH) Q4 2024

Management View

  • President and CEO Joe Zubretsky reported adjusted earnings per share at $5.05 for Q4 2024, with total premium revenue of $10 billion. He acknowledged the quarter fell short of expectations due to higher medical cost pressures in Medicaid and Medicare segments, contributing to a consolidated medical cost ratio (MCR) of 90.2% in Q4.
  • Full-year 2024 adjusted EPS reached $22.65, representing an 8.5% year-over-year increase, with premium revenue totaling $38.6 billion, up 19% year-over-year. The company maintained a pretax margin of 4.3%.
  • Management emphasized recent growth initiatives, including the acquisition of ConnectiCare, which is expected to add $1.2 billion in revenue primarily in the Marketplace segment. New contract wins in Georgia's Medicaid program and dual-eligible integrated products in multiple states are expected to contribute over $5 billion in incremental annual revenue.
  • CFO Mark Keim highlighted disciplined cost management, reporting a 6.7% G&A ratio for full-year 2024. He also addressed capital strength, noting $445 million in parent company cash and a debt-to-capital ratio of 41%.

Outlook

  • The company projects 2025 premium revenue of approximately $42 billion, reflecting 9% growth. Adjusted EPS guidance for 2025 is set at $24.50, an 8% increase year-over-year, with an embedded earnings realization of $1.50.
  • Medicaid MCR is forecasted at 89.9% for 2025, with most rate increases already known. Medicare is expected to see a slightly elevated MCR above target due to utilization pressure and suboptimal recent rates. Marketplace MCR is projected within target at 79%, with a pretax margin of 6%.
  • Management is optimistic about achieving $46 billion in premium revenue by 2026 and $52 billion by 2027, supported by recent contract wins and acquisitions.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 total revenue was approximately $10.5 billion, with premium revenue at $10 billion. Full-year revenue reached $38.6 billion, driven by 19% year-over-year growth.
  • Full-year consolidated MCR stood at 89.1%, with Medicaid at 90.3% and Medicare at 89.1%. Marketplace outperformed expectations with a full-year MCR of 75.4%, well below its long-term target range.
  • Operating cash flow for 2024 was $644 million, impacted by prior-year risk corridor settlements. The company repurchased 1.7 million shares in Q4, totaling $500 million.

Q&A

  • Andrew Mok, Barclays: Questioned Medicaid MCR guidance and rate trends. CEO Zubretsky explained the 2025 projection assumes a 4.5% rate increase matching a 4.5% trend, with half the rate increases already known. CFO Keim added that new store contributions and rate adjustments are expected to offset trend pressures.
  • Stephen Baxter, Wells Fargo: Asked about Q4 Medicaid MCR components. CFO Keim clarified that Q4 trend included higher utilization in LTSS, pharmacy, and behavioral health, with no material benefit from risk corridors.
  • Sarah James, Cantor Fitzgerald: Sought clarification on the 4.5% cost trend for 2025. Management reiterated confidence, noting that the trend assumption is conservative given the elevated baseline.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern about elevated cost trends in Medicaid and Medicare, as well as rate adequacy in 2025. Questions focused on the achievability of guidance given ongoing cost pressures.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing operational discipline and growth opportunities. CEO Zubretsky stated, "We are solidly on track to achieve the growth outlook we projected at our recent Investor Day."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more defensive, particularly regarding Medicaid MCR trends, while analysts appeared more skeptical about trend mitigation and rate adequacy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EPS declined from $6.01 in Q3 to $5.05 in Q4 due to higher medical costs. Full-year EPS guidance for 2024 was reaffirmed at $23.50.
  • Medicaid MCR increased from 90.5% in Q3 to 90.2% in Q4, with continued pressure from LTSS, pharmacy, and behavioral health utilization.
  • Marketplace performance remained strong, with a full-year MCR of 75.4% versus 73% in Q3, driven by lower-than-expected SEP membership costs.
  • Management's confidence in long-term growth initiatives remained consistent, but elevated cost trends in Medicaid and Medicare were more prominent concerns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Elevated medical cost trends in Medicaid and Medicare, particularly in LTSS, pharmacy, and behavioral health services, remain a challenge.
  • Dependence on state rate adjustments to align with cost trends introduces uncertainty. Management acknowledged the lag in rate adequacy but expressed confidence in ongoing advocacy.
  • Risk corridors provided limited benefit in Q4, raising concerns about their effectiveness as a financial buffer in 2025.

Final Takeaway

Molina Healthcare's Q4 2024 results reflected ongoing medical cost pressures, particularly in Medicaid and Medicare segments, which impacted short-term performance. However, management remains optimistic about long-term growth, supported by significant contract wins and the ConnectiCare acquisition. The company projects 9% revenue growth in 2025 to $42 billion, with adjusted EPS expected at $24.50. While cost trends and rate adequacy remain critical risks, Molina's disciplined cost management and focus on embedded earnings provide a foundation for sustained growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Warner Music targets high single-digit subscription revenue growth in fiscal 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Warner Music Group (NASDAQ:WMG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Kyncl emphasized the company's strategic goals of increasing market share, growing the total value of the music industry, and driving operational efficiency to enhance cash flow for reinvestment and shareholder returns. He highlighted key successes across new releases, reinvigorated catalogs, and notable market share growth, such as Atlantic Records increasing U.S. market share by 0.5 percentage points.
  • Warner Music made significant strategic moves, including the acquisition of a controlling interest in Tempo Music, which provides evergreen catalog rights with high margins and robust cash flow. The company also inked a new agreement with Spotify, aiming to further innovate and expand the music ecosystem.
  • CFO Bryan Castellani reported Q1 results adjusted for notable items, with total revenue up 4% and adjusted OIBDA rising 1%. He noted the impact of foreign exchange headwinds, which reduced adjusted OIBDA margin by approximately 200 basis points.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed expectations for high single-digit subscription streaming growth for fiscal 2025, supported by new digital service provider (DSP) agreements with Amazon and Spotify. Kyncl expressed confidence in the music industry's growth trajectory, citing opportunities to optimize pricing and expand into emerging markets.
  • The company is focused on increasing wholesale pricing efficiency and addressing legacy discounts in areas like family plans. Castellani noted that the foreign exchange headwinds currently affecting margins are expected to stabilize over time.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted Q1 revenue grew 4%, with recorded music revenue increasing 4% and music publishing revenue rising 7%. Subscription streaming revenue grew 7%, despite lapping last year's DSP price increases. Physical revenue increased 8%, driven by strong releases in the U.S. and Asia.
  • Adjusted OIBDA margin declined 80 basis points, primarily due to FX impacts and revenue mix. Operating cash flow increased 13% to $332 million, while free cash flow rose 12% to $296 million.
  • The company ended Q1 with $802 million in cash and $3.2 billion in net debt, maintaining a weighted average cost of debt of 4.2%.

Q&A

  • Benjamin Swinburne, Morgan Stanley: Asked about the structural evolution in the Spotify deal and its impact on revenue growth. Kyncl highlighted the company's three-pronged strategy and expressed satisfaction with progress on agreements with Spotify and Amazon.
  • Michael Morris, Guggenheim Securities LLC: Inquired about timing and scope of new fan experiences and subscription tiers. Kyncl confirmed that these initiatives are expected to roll out progressively and will vary by DSP.
  • Benjamin Black, Deutsche Bank: Raised concerns about TikTok's potential ban and its impact on ad-supported revenue. CFO Castellani noted that exposure would be minimal this year, with management focused on other growth areas.
  • Batya Levi, UBS: Asked about margin guidance under current FX conditions. Castellani stated that while FX headwinds would persist, operational efficiencies and cost management initiatives are expected to drive improvement over the long term.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a neutral to slightly positive sentiment, focusing on the potential of new agreements and revenue streams while expressing concerns over macroeconomic and FX impacts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in discussing strategic initiatives and subscription growth. Direct quotes like "we are confident about the future" and "this deal has growth built into it" reflected this optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone on FX challenges was more defensive, while analysts pressed more on profitability and growth sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • In Q1 2025, subscription streaming growth decelerated to 7% from Q4 2024's double-digit growth, reflecting the lapping of DSP price increases. Adjusted revenue growth of 4% marked a decline from Q4's normalized 6% growth.
  • Q1 exhibited a stronger focus on operational efficiency and strategic investments, such as the Tempo acquisition, compared to Q4's emphasis on structural reorganization and expansion in high-growth markets.
  • Analysts in Q1 2025 focused more on headwinds like FX and TikTok's potential ban, while Q4 2024 discussions leaned towards market share gains and strategic realignments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited FX headwinds as a significant short-term challenge, impacting adjusted OIBDA margins by approximately 200 basis points.
  • Analysts raised concerns about ad-supported revenue volatility, particularly related to macroeconomic pressures and uncertainties surrounding TikTok.
  • Emerging market expansions could face challenges due to low ARPU and penetration rates, though management remains optimistic about long-term growth potential.

Final Takeaway

Warner Music Group's Q1 2025 performance highlighted steady subscription streaming growth and strategic advancements, including landmark agreements with Spotify and the acquisition of Tempo Music. While FX headwinds and macroeconomic uncertainties present challenges, the company remains confident in its ability to drive long-term growth through operational efficiencies, pricing optimization, and a robust release slate. Investors should closely monitor the rollout of new subscription tiers and the impact of emerging market expansion on overall performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: FormFactor falls following Q4 results, guidance on lackluster PC, smartphone demand
data.description:
FormFactor (NASDAQ:FORM) fell more than 10% by noon trading on Thursday after its Q4 2024 financial results and guidance fell short of expectations.

"As expected, FormFactor reported sequentially lower fourth quarter revenue, gross margin and non-GAAP earnings per share, driven by the forecasted reduction in Foundry and Logic probe-card revenue," said FormFactor CEO Mike Slessor, in Wednesday night's earnings call. "This was partially offset by growth in DRAM probe-card revenue, which set a third consecutive quarterly record."

"On the one hand, the explosive growth of generative AI and high bandwidth memory, which more than quadrupled over 2023 and added $100M in revenue for FormFactor," he added. "On the other hand, lackluster demand persisted in important high unit volume markets like client PCs and mobile handsets."

The results prompted B. Riley Securities to downgrade the stock to Neutral from Buy, and slice its price target nearly in half to $34 from $64.

"FORM cited unusually weak PCs and smartphones as probe card demand culprits, and lower volume triggers a GM retreat back into the high 30s," said B. Riley analysts Craig Ellis and Stacy Che in a Thursday note. "We've observed high-volume application weakness but also believe large Foundry/Logic customer Intel (INTC) is tracking well below norms and now with uncertain recovery timing and magnitude potential."

data.title: Barrick's gold reserves rose in 2024 on Reko Diq project
data.description:
Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) said Thursday its overall proven and probable gold reserves increased 23% to 17.4M oz at the end of 2024, led by the conversion of resources from the Reko Diq copper-gold project to mineral reserves, adding 13M oz of gold at 0.28 g/t on an attributable basis, following the completion of the project's feasibility study.

Barrick (NYSE:GOLD), which considers Reko Diq as one of the world's largest underdeveloped copper-gold areas, owns a 50% stake in the mine and the governments of Pakistan and the province of Balochistan own rest.

Attributable measured and indicated gold resources for 2024 remain stable at 180M oz at 1.06 g/t, with another 41M oz at 0.9 g/t of inferred resources, up 5% from 2023.

The company said its gold mineral reserves in the Africa and Middle East region edged up ~1% to 19M oz at 3.35 g/t in 2024, driven by its Loulo-Gounkoto mine in Mali and the Bulyanhulu mine in Tanzania.

Barrick's (GOLD) copper mineral reserves jumped 224% Y/Y on an attributable basis to 18M metric tons of copper at 0.45% from 5.6M tons in 2023, due to the completion of the Lumwana and Reko Diq feasibility studies affirming both as Tier One copper projects.

data.title: Yum China targets 20,000 stores by 2026 with strong Q4 growth and margin expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Yum China (NYSE:YUMC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Joey Wat highlighted record-breaking achievements for 2024, including the opening of 1,751 net new stores, bringing the total to 16,395. Adjusted operating profit reached $1.2 billion, with diluted EPS growing 22% year-over-year. She emphasized operational efficiency and innovation as key drivers.
  • KFC expanded to over 2,200 cities in China, achieving 6% system sales growth for the year. Delivery sales rose 16%, and the KCOFFEE Cafe model grew from 50 cafes in 2023 to 700 by year-end 2024, with plans to reach 1,300 locations by the end of 2025.
  • Pizza Hut more than tripled its core operating profit in Q4 and achieved 19% annual growth, driven by a focus on affordability and operational efficiency. The WOW store model, with over 200 locations, demonstrated strong potential to attract younger customers and expand the addressable market.
  • Acting CFO Adrian Ding reported Q4 operating profit of $151 million, a 35% year-over-year increase. He noted significant cost savings through initiatives like Project Red Eye, which optimized procurement and ingredient usage.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its goal to reach 20,000 stores by 2026, with plans to open 1,600 to 1,800 net new stores in 2025.
  • Capital expenditure for 2025 is expected to range between $700 million and $800 million, consistent with 2024 levels.
  • Core operating profit margin is projected to remain stable or improve slightly in 2025, supported by favorable commodity prices and continuous efficiency gains.

Financial Results

  • Q4 system sales grew 4% year-over-year, with KFC and Pizza Hut achieving 5% and 3% system sales growth, respectively.
  • Restaurant margin for Pizza Hut expanded by 180 basis points year-over-year on a comparable basis, while KFC maintained a healthy margin profile with stable ticket averages.
  • Net income for Q4 was $115 million, a 17% year-over-year increase. Diluted EPS grew 27% to $0.30.
  • Full-year 2024 results included $740 million in free cash flow, enabling a 50% increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.24 per share.

Q&A

  • Michelle Cheng, Goldman Sachs: Asked about competitive landscape and pricing strategy. CEO Joey Wat noted modest price increases and rationalization of promotions, highlighting strong growth drivers for both KFC and Pizza Hut. She emphasized untapped market opportunities in lower-tier cities.
  • Chen Luo, Bank of America: Inquired about the revenue contribution from new smaller stores and franchise expansion. Acting CFO Adrian Ding explained that new stores typically achieve 50%-60% of mature store revenue in their first year. Franchise stores accounted for 30% of KFC’s net new openings in 2024.
  • Christine Peng, UBS: Asked about Pizza Hut WOW’s store economics. Acting CFO Adrian Ding noted that while WOW stores show promising transaction growth, there is room to improve delivery sales and margins.
  • Ethan Wang, CLSA: Questioned labor cost trends. Acting CFO Adrian Ding acknowledged headwinds from wage inflation and increasing delivery mix but highlighted ongoing efficiency initiatives to mitigate these pressures.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts persisted in raising concerns about revenue growth from smaller stores, competitive pricing, and cost pressure management. Questions reflected a slightly cautious tone regarding consumer spending and franchise expansion.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing resilience and robust strategies, particularly in operational efficiency and innovation. Phrases such as "we are confident" and "we are well-positioned" indicated optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone showed heightened focus on franchise expansion and new store models as key growth levers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize reaching 20,000 stores by 2026, compared to a broader focus on operational efficiency in Q3.
  • Strategic focus expanded to include aggressive franchise development, with franchise stores comprising 30% of KFC’s net new openings in Q4, an increase from 27% year-to-date in Q3.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused more on questions about margins and long-term revenue contributions from smaller stores, whereas Q3 discussions leaned toward pricing strategies and delivery growth.
  • Management’s confidence remained consistent, with additional emphasis on breakthrough models like Pizza Hut WOW and KCOFFEE cafes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential challenges from cautious consumer spending post-holidays and wage inflation in 2025.
  • Analysts flagged risks around maintaining efficiency gains amid franchise expansion and increasing delivery mix.
  • Management reaffirmed its commitment to maintaining food safety and operational integrity, emphasizing investments in digital supply chain visibility and a robust quality assurance framework.

Final Takeaway

Yum China delivered strong Q4 results, capping a record-breaking year with growth across key metrics. KFC and Pizza Hut continued to drive system sales and margin expansion, supported by innovations like KCOFFEE cafes and Pizza Hut WOW stores. With plans to open up to 1,800 stores in 2025 and a goal of 20,000 stores by 2026, the company remains focused on operational efficiency, innovation, and franchise development to sustain long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Lincoln National targets 2026 free cash flow conversion range of 45%-60%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lincoln National (NYSE:LNC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Ellen Cooper highlighted significant progress in 2024, achieving the highest full-year adjusted operating income in three years and surpassing financial objectives. She emphasized a strengthened capital foundation with an RBC ratio exceeding 430%, following strategic actions including the sale of the wealth management business and the launch of a Bermuda-based reinsurance subsidiary.

  • The group protection segment delivered record quarterly earnings, with margins doubling year-over-year to 8.4% and achieving a record year in sales, earnings, and margin expansion. Annuities also delivered robust results, with total sales reaching $3.7 billion in Q4, the highest since 2019. Retirement plan services achieved a 13% year-over-year earnings increase, supported by deposit growth of 25% for the full year.

  • CFO Christopher Neczypor reported Q4 adjusted operating income of $332 million or $1.91 per share, exceeding analysts' EPS estimate of $1.74. Net income available to common stockholders was $1.7 billion, primarily driven by favorable market risk benefits and derivatives gains tied to higher interest rates.

Outlook

  • Management increased the top end of its 2026 free cash flow conversion target to a range of 45%-60%, citing ongoing mix shifts toward higher-margin businesses and optimizing capital efficiency.

  • The group protection margin is expected to remain flat in 2025 compared to 2024, given anticipated normalization of macro tailwinds. However, the margin is projected to exceed 8% by 2026 due to strategic initiatives and improved mortality trends.

  • Annuities are positioned for continued growth through a diversified product mix and enhanced capital efficiency, supported by the Bermuda reinsurance affiliate.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted operating income per share came in at $1.91 vs. analysts' estimate of $1.74. Revenues for Q4, however, were not explicitly disclosed in the transcript for direct comparison to the $4.72 billion estimate.

  • Group protection achieved an 8.4% margin, more than doubling year-over-year, driven by disciplined pricing, operational efficiencies, and favorable macroeconomic conditions.

  • Annuities reported Q4 operating income of $303 million, up from $279 million in the prior year, supported by higher account balances and increased spread income. Retirement plan services recorded a 13% earnings increase year-over-year, with first-year sales rising 46%.

  • Life insurance faced challenges, reporting a $15 million operating loss due to elevated mortality severity, though management does not expect this to persist.

Q&A

  • Ryan Krueger, KBW: Asked about the raised free cash flow outlook for 2026 and its drivers. CFO Neczypor cited ongoing mix shifts, optimizing sales capital efficiency, and legacy block actions as key contributors.

  • Suneet Kamath, Jefferies: Inquired about the role of the Bermuda subsidiary. CFO Neczypor emphasized its focus on improving capital efficiency for new sales and potential future internal reinsurance opportunities.

  • Dan Bergman, TD Cowen: Questioned group protection premium growth and competitive pricing. CEO Cooper highlighted full-year premium growth of 3%, reflecting disciplined pricing and investments in operations and technology.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with questions centered on sustainability of free cash flow growth and operational execution. Skepticism was evident regarding the longevity of macroeconomic tailwinds.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly on strategic execution and long-term profitability goals. Phrases like "we are confident" and "we believe" reinforced their positive outlook.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained steady, while analysts showed increased interest in capital efficiency and margin sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted operating income per share decreased to $1.91 from $2.06 in Q3. However, the quarter saw stronger group protection performance and significant annuity sales growth.

  • Management maintained its focus on margin improvement and capital efficiency, with increased clarity on 2026 free cash flow targets.

  • Analysts continued to emphasize macroeconomic impacts and reinsurance strategies, similar to the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Elevated mortality severity in life insurance posed a challenge, though management expects normalization.

  • Macro tailwinds driving group protection margins may not persist, potentially impacting near-term profitability.

  • Increased competition in the RILA market and potential volatility in annuity sales could affect growth trajectories.

Final Takeaway

Lincoln National demonstrated strong performance in Q4 2024, supported by record results in group protection and robust annuity sales. Strategic initiatives, including the Bermuda reinsurance affiliate, are expected to drive long-term profitability. Management raised its 2026 free cash flow conversion target, signaling confidence in its operational strategies, though macroeconomic conditions and competitive pressures remain key risks.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Integrating diverse asset classes is the new ‘cornerstone’ of investing – KKR
data.description:
Public and private markets are converging asset classes to raise cash, creating an “iPhone moment” for 2025 credit, according to private equity and investment company KKR & Co.

In an investor letter, Christopher Sheldon, co-head of credit and markets, and Tal Reback, director of credit and markets at KKR & Co., said the cornerstone of a modern portfolio strategy is integrating diverse asset classes in the debt and equity markets, Bloomberg reported.

“The global credit markets are undergoing their own transformation,” they said, and compared it to the launch of the iPhone. It “wasn’t just the debut of a new product, it was the dawn of a new paradigm.”

“The old paradigm of siloed, fragmented products is giving way to a new era of diversified income solutions across multi-asset credit platforms,” the letter said. “These solutions represent the ‘iPhone moment.’”

KKR & Co. oversees about $247B of credit assets.

KKR & Co. is also investing in Asia-Pacific credit, which offers higher yields and risk-adjusted returns compared to credits in developed markets.

According to the letter, Asia-Pacific high-yield debt returned 16.4% to investors on an annualized basis. U.S. high-yield debt, on the other hand, returned 8.2%.

“We suspect that Asia-Pacific may leapfrog ahead of the U.S.” Sheldon and Reback wrote.

The U.S. will join Asia-Pacific to integrate diversified multi-asset investments, “capitalizing on opportunities to achieve relative value without taking on incremental risk.”

High yield ETFs: (VYM), (NYSEARCA:HYG), (JNK), (VYMI)

Asia Pacific high yield ETFs: (IAE), (KHYB)

Treasury yields: (US10Y), (US30Y), (US5Y), (US6M), (US12M)

TIPS: (5YTIPS), (10YTIPS), (30YTIPS)

Broad bonds: (SPLB), (GBF), (BND), (SPIB), (SPIB), (BSV)

U.S. Gov. bonds: (GOVI), (SPTL), (TIP), (SPTI), (BIL)

Corporate bonds: (IGLB), (PFIG), (IGIB), (PHB), (IGSB)

International bonds: (PCY), (WIP), (BWX), (BWZ), (PICB)

Here is Seeking Alpha’s list of bond ETFs and tickers.

data.title: Aptiv targets $31B in new business awards for 2025 while advancing EV and ADAS growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aptiv PLC (NYSE:APTV) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Kevin P. Clark highlighted Aptiv's strong finish to 2024, achieving $31 billion in new business bookings, including a record $10.1 billion in Q4. Growth was driven by advanced safety technologies and electrical distribution systems, with $7 billion in new business from China alone.
  • The company reported Q4 revenue of $4.9 billion, reflecting a 1% decline due to lower production schedules in Europe and China. Operating income reached $623 million, supported by cost reductions and share repurchases, driving EPS growth of 25%.
  • Clark emphasized strategic milestones, including launches of Gen 6 ADAS platforms, integrated cockpit controllers, and Wind River's Elixir Pro, which has gained traction with global partners like AWS and Intel.
  • CFO Varun Laroyia noted record operating cash flow of $1.1 billion, enabling significant debt paydown and supporting Aptiv's deleveraging efforts.

Outlook

  • Aptiv projects 2025 revenue in the range of $19.6 billion to $20.4 billion, reflecting 2% growth at the midpoint. Adjusted EPS is forecasted at $7.00 to $7.60, with operating income expected to reach $2.42 billion.
  • The company anticipates robust growth in active safety revenues, up high single digits, and electrified platform revenues, up low double digits. However, guidance incorporates additional conservatism for North American production due to geopolitical uncertainties.
  • Management reiterated confidence in achieving over $31 billion in new business awards in 2025, supported by continued electrification and advanced ADAS adoption globally.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 came in at $1.75, surpassing analysts' estimates of $1.65. Revenue of $4.9 billion was in line with expectations.
  • Active safety revenues grew mid-teens, driven by strong North American performance, while user experience revenues declined due to legacy program roll-offs.
  • Wind River's revenues grew 14% in Q4 but faced full-year declines due to slower 5G investment and extended sales cycles in automotive and industrial markets.
  • Signal and Power Solutions revenue declined 3%, impacted by weaker production schedules in North America and Europe. Non-automotive markets, however, showed 5% growth.

Q&A

  • John Murphy, Bank of America, asked about incremental margins if North American production exceeds forecasts. Kevin P. Clark estimated flow-through margins in the 18%-22% range but emphasized the conservative approach in guidance due to market uncertainties.
  • Joseph Spak, UBS, inquired about adjustments to global EV production forecasts. Clark noted a more cautious outlook, with EV revenue growth expected at mid-single digits versus IHS projections of 20%.
  • Dan Levy, Barclays, questioned cost reduction initiatives. Clark highlighted ongoing salary reductions, footprint rotations, and material cost negotiations, with additional efforts planned for 2025.
  • Emmanuel Rosner, Deutsche Bank, asked about mitigating potential tariff impacts. Management outlined regionalization efforts, with 90% of manufacturing and 80% of sourcing already localized.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern over conservatism in guidance and production volatility, but acknowledged Aptiv’s proactive cost management measures.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing strong execution despite market headwinds. Statements like "we remain confident" reflected confidence in the long-term strategy.
  • Compared to Q3, there was increased caution in addressing geopolitical risks and customer production schedules.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue decline of 1% showed modest improvement from Q3's 6% drop, reflecting stabilization in production schedules.
  • EPS growth of 25% in Q4 was achieved despite ongoing challenges, compared to Q3's 41% growth driven by one-time restructuring benefits.
  • Management's tone in Q4 was more cautious, reflecting heightened focus on geopolitical risks and market uncertainties.
  • Analysts continued to question production volatility and EV growth, consistent with Q3 concerns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited geopolitical uncertainties, including potential tariff changes and North American production risks, as key challenges for 2025.
  • Slower EV adoption and production schedule volatility remain primary risks, particularly in Europe and North America.
  • Analysts highlighted concerns about profitability pressures in China due to shorter product cycles and high upfront investments.

Final Takeaway

Aptiv showcased strong operational performance in Q4 2024, supported by record business bookings and robust cash flow, while strategically positioning itself for growth in advanced ADAS and electrification markets. Despite near-term conservatism in guidance, the company remains confident in its ability to navigate market uncertainties and capitalize on long-term growth opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
